title: Signal completion
description:
Work is done. Signal the controller to reclaim this session:

```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
```

Run this command and nothing else.

title: Read assignment, implement, and close
description:
You have been assigned a work bead. Read it, do the work, and close it.

**1. Read your assignment:**
```bash
bd show ev-6ux
```

Read the bead's title and description carefully. This is your task.

**2. Implement the solution:**

Do exactly what the bead describes. Follow existing codebase conventions.
Make atomic, focused commits as you work:
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description>"
```

**3. Close the bead when done:**
```bash
bd update ev-6ux --status=closed --notes "Done: <brief summary of what you did>"
```

If you get stuck or need clarification, check your inbox:
```bash
gc mail inbox
```

**Exit criteria:** The work described in the bead is complete and the bead is closed.

title: mol-do-work
description:
Simple work formula — read the bead, do what it says, close it.

This is the minimal work lifecycle for coding agents. No git branching,
no worktree isolation, no refinery handoff. The agent reads the bead's
description, implements the solution in the current working directory,
and closes the bead when done.

Use this for demos and simple single-agent workflows. For production
multi-agent setups with branch isolation and merge review, use
mol-polecat-work instead.

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | caller | The work bead ID assigned to this agent |

title: DEL-91: complete capture-web impl, tests, and open PR
description:
## Context

Jira: https://riddim.atlassian.net/browse/DEL-91
Repo: RiddimSoftware/evidence
Branch: claude/del-91-capture-web
Worktree: /Users/sunny/code/evidence-del-91 (already exists on the right branch)

## What is already done (do NOT re-implement)

In /Users/sunny/code/evidence-del-91:
- Sources/EvidenceCLIKit/EvidenceCLI.swift — captureWeb(), resolveViewport(), pageSlug() implemented; `node` field added to ToolPaths
- Sources/EvidenceCLIKit/Resources/capture-web.js — Playwright Node script (untracked, needs staging)
- Package.swift — Resources bundle for capture-web.js already added
- DEL-90 (config story) is merged to origin/main; worktree is already on that commit

## What still needs to be done

Work in /Users/sunny/code/evidence-del-91 (cd there first).

### 1. Update Help.swift (Sources/EvidenceCLIKit/Help.swift)

a) In `root`, add after the upload-screenshots line in the Commands list:
      capture-web           Capture full-page Playwright screenshots at configured viewport sizes.

b) Add static property captureWeb (before the text(for:) function):
```
public static let captureWeb = """
evidence capture-web

Captures full-page web screenshots at each configured viewport using Playwright.

Requires platform = "web" in .evidence.toml plus:
  web_url          = "https://example.com"
  web_viewports    = ["desktop-1440", "mobile-390"]

Named viewport presets:
  desktop-1440  -> 1440x900
  mobile-390    -> 390x844
  Custom WxH strings (e.g. "1280x800") are also accepted.

Optional:
  web_full_page   = true           (default true)
  web_wait_until  = "networkidle"  (default networkidle)

Output: <evidence_dir>/<viewport-name>/<page-slug>.png

Requires:
  node (with playwright installed: npm install playwright)

Example:
  evidence capture-web
"""
```

c) In text(for:), add before `default`:
   case "capture-web": captureWeb

### 2. Write tests in Tests/EvidenceCLIKitTests/EvidenceCLIKitTests.swift

Read the existing test file first — especially the testCLI helper at the bottom and how MockCommandRunner is used — then add these tests before the private func testCLI line:

a) func testResolveViewportPresets()
   - XCTAssertEqual(EvidenceCLI.resolveViewport("desktop-1440"), "1440x900")
   - XCTAssertEqual(EvidenceCLI.resolveViewport("mobile-390"), "390x844")
   - XCTAssertEqual(EvidenceCLI.resolveViewport("1280x800"), "1280x800")
   - XCTAssertEqual(EvidenceCLI.resolveViewport("custom"), "custom")

b) func testPageSlugDerivation()
   - root/empty URL -> "index"
   - /about -> "about"
   - /about/team -> "about-team"
   - invalid string -> "index"

c) func testCaptureWebRunsNodeScriptForEachViewport() — using MockCommandRunner (follow the exact same pattern as existing tests that use it)
   - Web platform config (platform=web, web_url=http://localhost:8765, web_viewports=["desktop-1440","mobile-390"])
   - Mock runner returns exitCode=0, creates dummy output files at expected paths so fileExists check passes
   - Invoke via testCLI or directly
   - Assert node was invoked twice
   - Assert first call has "1440x900", second has "390x844"

d) func testCaptureWebRejectsIOSPlatform() — iOS config, capture-web throws CLIError.usage

### 3. Integration test

Add at end of the test class:
```swift
func testCaptureWebIntegration() throws {
    // Skip if node not available
    let nodePath = "/usr/local/bin/node"
    try XCTSkipIf(!FileManager.default.isExecutableFile(atPath: nodePath),
                  "node not found at \(nodePath)")

    // Skip if playwright not installed (check via node -e require('playwright'))
    let checkProc = Process()
    checkProc.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: nodePath)
    checkProc.arguments = ["-e", "require('playwright')"]
    try checkProc.run()
    checkProc.waitUntilExit()
    try XCTSkipIf(checkProc.terminationStatus != 0, "playwright not installed")

    // Locate bundled script
    guard let scriptURL = Bundle.module.url(forResource: "capture-web", withExtension: "js") else {
        XCTFail("capture-web.js not in bundle")
        return
    }

    // Write fixture HTML to temp dir
    let tmpDir = FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory.appendingPathComponent(UUID().uuidString)
    try FileManager.default.createDirectory(at: tmpDir, withIntermediateDirectories: true)
    defer { try? FileManager.default.removeItem(at: tmpDir) }
    let htmlFile = tmpDir.appendingPathComponent("index.html")
    try "<html><body style='height:3000px;background:red'>hello</body></html>".write(to: htmlFile, atomically: true, encoding: .utf8)

    // Start HTTP server
    let port = 18765
    let server = Process()
    server.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/usr/bin/python3")
    server.arguments = ["-m", "http.server", "\(port)", "--directory", tmpDir.path]
    server.standardOutput = FileHandle.nullDevice
    server.standardError = FileHandle.nullDevice
    try server.run()
    defer { server.terminate() }
    Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval: 1.0)

    // Run capture
    let outputFile = tmpDir.appendingPathComponent("out.png")
    let capture = Process()
    capture.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: nodePath)
    capture.arguments = [scriptURL.path, "http://localhost:\(port)/", "1440x900", "true", "networkidle", outputFile.path]
    try capture.run()
    capture.waitUntilExit()
    XCTAssertEqual(capture.terminationStatus, 0, "capture-web.js exited non-zero")

    // Check PNG magic bytes
    XCTAssertTrue(FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: outputFile.path), "PNG not created")
    let data = try Data(contentsOf: outputFile)
    XCTAssertGreaterThanOrEqual(data.count, 8)
    let magic: [UInt8] = [137, 80, 78, 71, 13, 10, 26, 10]
    let header = Array(data.prefix(8)).map { UInt8($0) }
    XCTAssertEqual(header, magic, "Not a valid PNG file")
}
```

### 4. Run swift test

```bash
cd /Users/sunny/code/evidence-del-91
swift test 2>&1
```

Fix any failures. The integration test may be skipped if playwright is not installed — that is acceptable.

### 5. Commit

```bash
cd /Users/sunny/code/evidence-del-91
git add -A
git status  # confirm only DEL-91 related files
git diff --cached --stat
git commit -m "[DEL-91]: implement capture-web subcommand (Playwright, named viewports, full-page)"
```

### 6. Rebase and push

```bash
git fetch origin main
git rebase origin/main
git push -u origin claude/del-91-capture-web
git rev-list --count origin/main..HEAD  # must be >= 1
```

### 7. Open PR as developer-bot

Use dev-bot-gh.sh (NOT plain gh):
```bash
/Users/sunny/code/agent-config/scripts/dev-bot-gh.sh pr create \
  --repo RiddimSoftware/evidence \
  --title "[DEL-91]: implement capture-web subcommand (Playwright, named viewports, full-page)" \
  --label autonomous \
  --body "## Summary

- Adds evidence capture-web subcommand: reads .evidence.toml with platform = web, invokes bundled capture-web.js via Node/Playwright, writes one PNG per viewport to evidence_dir/viewport-name/page-slug.png
- Named viewport presets: desktop-1440 -> 1440x900, mobile-390 -> 390x844. Custom WxH strings pass through.
- web_full_page and web_wait_until forwarded to Playwright. Non-zero exit on tool not found, unreachable URL, or write failure.
- Help text updated for capture-web.
- Unit tests: resolveViewport, pageSlug, command flow, wrong-platform guard.
- Integration test: serves HTML fixture, runs capture-web, PNG magic-bytes check.

Closes DEL-91. Depends on DEL-90 (merged as PR #18).

## Test plan
- [x] swift test passes (unit + integration)
- [x] PR opened with autonomous label for auto-merge"
```

Then immediately set automerge on the PR number returned:
```bash
gh pr merge <NUMBER> --auto --squash
```

### 8. Post PR URL to Jira DEL-91

Post a Jira comment on DEL-91 with the PR URL. Use the Atlassian Rovo MCP (addCommentToJiraIssue) or:
```bash
export AWS_PROFILE=bettrack
JIRA_TOKEN=$(aws secretsmanager get-secret-value --secret-id jira/api-token --region us-east-1 --query SecretString --output text)
curl -s -X POST "https://riddim.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/issue/DEL-91/comment" \
  -H "Authorization: Basic $(echo -n sunny@riddimsoftware.com:$JIRA_TOKEN | base64)" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"body":{"type":"doc","version":1,"content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"PR: <URL>"}]}]}}'
```

## Definition of done

Bead closed only after: swift test passes, PR is open with autonomous label, automerge is set, PR URL is posted to Jira DEL-91.
close_reason: DEL-91 complete: PR opened and automerge set. PR: https://github.com/RiddimSoftware/evidence/pull/19

Simple work formula — read the bead, do what it says, close it.

This is the minimal work lifecycle for coding agents. No git branching,
no worktree isolation, no refinery handoff. The agent reads the bead's

title: [DEL-91] implement capture-web subcommand (Playwright, named viewports, full-page)
description:
## Jira ticket
[DEL-91](https://riddim.atlassian.net/browse/DEL-91): evidence: implement capture-web subcommand

## Your role for this work
You are an Autonomous Developer. Follow the full AD contract:
- Create a git worktree at /Users/sunny/code/evidence/worktrees/del-91
- Branch: claude/del-91-capture-web off origin/main
- Implement per the AC below
- swift test must pass including the integration test
- Open PR via: gh pr create --label autonomous --title '[DEL-91]: implement capture-web subcommand (Playwright, named viewports, full-page)' --body-file -
- Post PR URL as a Jira comment on DEL-91
- Write exit state to /Users/sunny/code/.agent-state/dev-done/DEL-91.json
- Close this bead and run: gc runtime drain-ack && exit

## Target repo
RiddimSoftware/evidence at /Users/sunny/code/evidence

## Architecture decision (from Spike DEL-89)
Option A selected: Swift CLI shells out to Node.js.
evidence capture-web spawns a Node.js subprocess that runs a Playwright script.
Node.js + playwright package must be on PATH when the command runs.

## Key files
- Sources/EvidenceCLIKit/EvidenceCLI.swift — add capture-web subcommand
- Sources/EvidenceCLIKit/CommandRunner.swift — add web capture runner logic
- Tests/ — add integration test

## Proof-of-concept reference (from riddim-website PR #25 review)
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
  const page = await browser.newPage();
  await page.setViewportSize({ width: 1440, height: 900 });
  await page.goto(url, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });
  const screenshot = await page.screenshot({ fullPage: true });
Use page.setViewportSize() ONLY — do NOT use browser window resize.

## Acceptance criteria
1. evidence capture-web reads .evidence.toml, validates platform = "web", captures one PNG per viewport
2. Output: <evidence_dir>/<viewport-name>/<page-slug>.png (slug from web_url path, defaults to "index" for root)
3. Named presets: desktop-1440 = 1440x900, mobile-390 = 390x844. Custom WxH strings (e.g. "1280x800") also work
4. web_full_page = true captures full scrollable page height. false captures only viewport height
5. web_wait_until forwarded to Playwright waitUntil option
6. Exits non-zero with clear error: Playwright binary not found / web_url unreachable / write fails
7. Exits non-zero if platform != "web" (wrong subcommand)
8. Integration test: serve a static HTML fixture locally, run capture-web, verify output PNGs exist with valid PNG magic bytes
9. evidence --help lists capture-web with a one-line description
10. swift test passes

## Key constraints
- Output dir <evidence_dir>/<viewport-name>/ must be created if it doesn't exist
- Fail with clear error if parent evidence_dir is unwritable
- Do NOT add nested TOML table parsing — use flat web_* keys (already implemented in DEL-90/PR #18)

## Definition of Done
PR merged to RiddimSoftware/evidence. swift test passes including integration test.
close_reason: Superseded by ev-6ux — worktree already existed at evidence-del-91 with partial implementation; ev-6ux has corrected instructions
